literal: strand, cord, constituent (root sense: a strand of a rope); in later technical usage 'quality, attribute', but the SńĀŠĻÉkhya register preserves the strand-of-prakŠĻõti sense and the VaiŇõeŠĻ£ika register the property-of-substance sense ‚ÄĒ these are different metaphysical registers that English flattens
invariant_definition:
A *guŠĻáa* is not 'a quality' in the Western analytic sense of an accidental predicate inhering in a substance. Two distinct technical registers must be held apart, because the VedńĀntic schools work primarily within the first while continuing to read texts that operate in the second.

(1) **SńĀŠĻÉkhya register (the dominant VedńĀntic register).** The three *guŠĻáas* ‚ÄĒ *sattva* (lucid, ordered, illuminating), *rajas* (active, energizing, passion-producing), *tamas* (heavy, obscuring, inertial) ‚ÄĒ are the three ontological strands or constituent fabrics of *prakŠĻõti* itself. They are not properties *of* the world; they are what the world is made of. *SńĀŠĻÉkhya-KńĀrikńĀ* 11‚Äď13 describes their joint operation under the rope-strand metaphor: the manifest is woven from three strands that mutually suppress and support one another, the dominance-ratio at any locus determining the character of that locus. The *guŠĻáas* are *bhautika* in a strict sense ‚ÄĒ substantial constituents, not adjectives. This is why *guŠĻáa-traya*, *traiguŠĻáya*, *guŠĻáńĀtńęta*, *nirguŠĻáa*, *saguŠĻáa* all carry weight that 'three qualities', 'tri-quality', 'beyond qualities', 'without qualities', 'with qualities' cannot bear: the Sanskrit terms reach into the constitutive metaphysics of *prakŠĻõti*, while the English glosses suggest mere predicate-logic.

(2) **NyńĀya-VaiŇõeŠĻ£ika register.** *GuŠĻáa* is one of the six (or seven) *padńĀrtha*s of VaiŇõeŠĻ£ika categorial ontology ‚ÄĒ a real but dependent property-substance inhering by *samavńĀya* in *dravya*. Here 'quality' is a defensible English gloss because the VaiŇõeŠĻ£ika grid of *padńĀrtha*s is itself a property-of-substance scheme. The twenty-four *guŠĻáa*s of classical VaiŇõeŠĻ£ika (color, taste, smell, touch, number, magnitude, separateness, conjunction, disjunction, priority, posteriority, weight, fluidity, viscidity, sound, *buddhi*, *sukha*, *duŠł•kha*, *icchńĀ*, *dveŠĻ£a*, *prayatna*, *dharma*, *adharma*, *saŠĻÉskńĀra*) are inhering properties, not constitutive strands.

In VedńĀnta the two registers are constantly in play. When a school says *brahman* is *nirguŠĻáa*, the question 'in which register?' is doctrinally decisive ‚ÄĒ see the entries on *nirguŠĻáa* and *saguŠĻáa* for the sub-school analysis.

per_school.definition: At the *vyńĀvahńĀrika* level, Advaita treats *prakŠĻõti* as *triguŠĻáńĀtmikńĀ* and distinguishes *mńĀyńĀ* from *avidyńĀ* through the purity and impurity of *sattva* within that same guŠĻáic matrix [[Pa√ĪcadaŇõńę 1.15-16](cite://vidyaranya/panchadashi/1.15-16)]. In MadhusŇędana's devotional Advaita, hearing a text woven from the grandeur of BhagavńĀn's *guŠĻáas* is the direct *sńĀdhana* of *bhakti*, whereas the *mahńĀvńĀkya* is the *sńĀdhana* of *brahma-vidyńĀ* [[BR 1.1 ŠĻ≠ńękńĀ](cite://madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.ŠĻ≠ńękńĀ.1)] [[BR 1.1 ŠĻ≠ńękńĀ.6](cite://madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ŠĻ≠ńękńĀ.6)].

RńĀmńĀnuja takes *brahman* to denote *PuruŠĻ£ottama*, whose *svarŇępa* is intrinsically free from all *doŠĻ£a* and constituted by an unsurpassed, innumerable host of *kalyńĀŠĻáa-guŠĻáas*; even the lexical force of *brahman* is explained through greatness in *svarŇępa* and in *guŠĻáa* [[Ňörńę BhńĀŠĻ£ya 1.1.1, comm. 6-8](cite://ramanuja/shri-bhasya/6)] [[Ňörńę BhńĀŠĻ£ya 1.1.1, comm. 7](cite://ramanuja/shri-bhasya/7)] [[Ňörńę BhńĀŠĻ£ya 1.1.1, comm. 8](cite://ramanuja/shri-bhasya/8)]. The *jńęva* is described as *j√ĪńĀnńĀnandaika-guŠĻáa*, while the *antaryńĀmin* NńĀrńĀyaŠĻáa is the *anavadhikńĀtiŇõayńĀsaŠĻÉkhyeya-kalyńĀŠĻáa-guŠĻáagaŠĻáa* who qualifies and governs the whole *cid-acid*-complex by *sńĀmńĀnńĀdhikaraŠĻáya* and inner-selfhood [[VedńĀrtha-SaŠĻÉgraha ¬ß5](cite://ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/¬ß5)] [[VedńĀrtha-SaŠĻÉgraha ¬ß6](cite://ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/¬ß6)].

Madhva's school treats ViŠĻ£ŠĻáu as *sarvaguŠĻáa-pŇęrŠĻáa* and *doŠĻ£a-varjita*, and explicitly refuses any separation of divine *guŠĻáa* from divine *svarŇępa*: *guŠĻáa-kriyńĀdayo viŠĻ£ŠĻáoŠł• svarŇępam* [[BBsBh 1.1.1.1](cite://madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.1)] [[VTV 3.12](cite://madhva/visnu-tattva-vinirnaya/3.12)]. The Lord's displayed embodiment never compromises those pure auspicious *guŠĻáas* [[VTV 3.9](cite://madhva/visnu-tattva-vinirnaya/3.9)]. The liberated *jńęva* retains *cid-ńĀnanda*-beginning *guŠĻáas* only by *sńĀdŠĻõŇõya*, so *abheda*-language marks likeness and dependence, never *svarŇępńĀbheda* with ń™Ňõvara [[Anuvy. 2.3.66-69](cite://madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3.66-69)] [[Anuvy. 2.3.67](cite://madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3.67)].

Jńęva GosvńĀmin construes the *jńęva*-multitude as standing, by BhagavńĀn's *svńĀbhńĀvika-tad-acintya-Ňõakti*, in a relation of *guŠĻáa-sthńĀnńęyatva* to him, like ray-atoms to the sun; *abheda-ŇõńĀstra* therefore requires simultaneous preservation of *vyatireka* and *avyatireka* [[ParamńĀtma-Sandarbha ¬ß43](cite://jiva-gosvami/sat-sandarbha/¬ß43)]. BhagavńĀn alone is the final *prayojana* because he is *sarvńĀdhika-guŠĻáa-ŇõńĀlin*, the unsurpassed locus of supreme qualities and thus of *parama-prema* [[ParamńĀtma-Sandarbha ¬ß45](cite://jiva-gosvami/sat-sandarbha/¬ß45)].

NimbńĀrka's meditation-verse defines the supreme Brahman as KŠĻõŠĻ£ŠĻáa who is by nature devoid of all *doŠĻ£a* and the single mass of inexhaustible *kalyńĀŠĻáa-guŠĻáas* [[DaŇõa-Ňölokńę 4](cite://nimbarka/dasa-shloki/4)]. The Lord's *brahmatva*, *vyŇęha*-form, and auspicious *guŠĻáa*-plenitude are presented together as one indivisible object of contemplation [[DaŇõa-Ňölokńę 4](cite://nimbarka/dasa-shloki/4)].

translator_note: Translating *guŠĻáa* uniformly as 'quality' is the foundational lazy collapse from which a chain of downstream errors follows. The SńĀŠĻÉkhya-derived register ‚ÄĒ where *guŠĻáa* names a constitutive strand of *prakŠĻõti* ‚ÄĒ is the dominant VedńĀntic register. In that register *guŠĻáa* is no more a 'quality' than a strand of a rope is a 'quality' of the rope. When the gloss 'quality' is then carried into *nirguŠĻáa* and *saguŠĻáa*, the resulting English ('without qualities', 'with qualities') reads as a flat predicate-logic claim and severs the term from the metaphysics it lives in. Acceptable practice: leave *guŠĻáa* untranslated; on first use specify the register (SńĀŠĻÉkhya-strand vs. VaiŇõeŠĻ£ika-padńĀrtha vs. VaiŠĻ£ŠĻáava-kalyńĀŠĻáa); never translate *guŠĻáńĀtńęta* as 'beyond qualities' (use 'beyond the three *guŠĻáa*s' or 'beyond the strands of *prakŠĻõti*'); never gloss *nirguŠĻáa* as 'qualityless' or *saguŠĻáa* as 'qualified' without qualification.
